name_entries.name: Unmarkets
name_entries.felt_sense: When you notice that the most helpful organizations are the ones trying to make themselves unnecessary — and you don't have a word for that economic logic
name_entries.definition: Economic space where loss is internalized not externalized
name_entries.why_it_matters: Central concept from Aaron Titus - organizations succeed by becoming less needed
name_entries.key_concepts: Loss internalization, coordination not consumption, free infrastructure

name_entries.name: Platform Commons
name_entries.felt_sense: When infrastructure serves you but you have no say in how it works — and you sense there should be a third option between privatized and abandoned
name_entries.definition: Infrastructure maintained by organization but controlled by community
name_entries.why_it_matters: Gifted Dreamers' governance model for infrastructure
name_entries.key_concepts: Shared maintenance, community control, prevents capture

name_entries.name: Loss Internalization
name_entries.felt_sense: When a community gets strong enough that it no longer needs the organization that helped it — and you recognize that as success, not failure
name_entries.definition: Communities absorb capacity reducing need for external organizations
name_entries.why_it_matters: Success metric for Unmarkets approach

name_entries.name: Shalom
name_entries.felt_sense: When 'peace' feels too thin a word for what you actually want — something more like wholeness, where relationships and justice are included
name_entries.definition: Hebrew concept of wholeness and right relationships
name_entries.why_it_matters: Kristine's theological framework for community vision

name_entries.name: Interdependence
name_entries.felt_sense: When you need others but you're also complete in yourself — and neither 'independent' nor 'codependent' captures that
name_entries.definition: We need each other AND we're whole - not independence or codependence
name_entries.why_it_matters: Framework for understanding healthy community relationships

name_entries.name: Prefiguration
name_entries.felt_sense: When you realize the way you're building something matters as much as what you're building — the process is the product
name_entries.definition: The means must embody the ends we seek
name_entries.why_it_matters: Principle that infrastructure design embodies desired world
name_entries.key_concepts: Practice what you preach, embody values, direct action

name_entries.name: Praxis
name_entries.felt_sense: When thinking without acting feels hollow, and acting without reflecting feels reckless — you need the spiral of both
name_entries.definition: Integrated practice of reflection and action for transformation
name_entries.why_it_matters: Framework for learning/doing/being as unified practice
name_entries.key_concepts: Reflection-action spiral, learning by doing, consciousness

name_entries.name: Commons
name_entries.felt_sense: When a resource belongs to everyone but is governed by no one, and you sense it needs community rules to survive
name_entries.definition: Resources + community + governance rules managed collectively
name_entries.why_it_matters: Alternative to privatization and enclosure
name_entries.key_concepts: Collective management, shared resources, community governance

name_entries.name: Commoning
name_entries.felt_sense: When you realize 'the commons' isn't a thing that exists but a practice people do together — maintaining shared resources requires ongoing work
name_entries.definition: Active process of creating and maintaining commons
name_entries.why_it_matters: Understanding commons as verb not noun

name_entries.name: Enclosure
name_entries.felt_sense: When something that belonged to everyone quietly becomes someone's property — and you feel the loss even if you can't name it
name_entries.definition: Process of privatizing common resources and removing community control
name_entries.why_it_matters: Understanding how commons have been destroyed

name_entries.name: Cultivator Consciousness
name_entries.felt_sense: When fighting metaphors exhaust you and you want language for tending conditions instead of winning battles
name_entries.definition: Creating conditions for flourishing vs warrior mindset of conquering
name_entries.why_it_matters: Alternative to war frame for transformation work
name_entries.key_concepts: Cultivation not combat, conditions for emergence, sustainability

name_entries.name: Regenerative vs Extractive
name_entries.felt_sense: When you can feel whether a system is building capacity or depleting it — and you need a lens to make that intuition legible
name_entries.definition: Systems that increase vs decrease capacity to thrive
name_entries.why_it_matters: Lens for evaluating whether systems build or deplete
name_entries.key_concepts: Living systems, increased capacity, reciprocity vs depletion

name_entries.name: Meaning Through Practice
name_entries.felt_sense: When someone asks 'what's the meaning of this?' and you realize the answer only comes from doing the work, not from a framework
name_entries.definition: Meaning emerges from engaged action not predetermined framework
name_entries.why_it_matters: Response to Kristine's question about meaning-making

name_entries.name: Fractals
name_entries.felt_sense: When you notice the same pattern at the team level, the organization level, and the movement level — and realize it's not coincidence
name_entries.definition: Patterns that repeat at different scales
name_entries.why_it_matters: Principle that microcosm reflects macrocosm

name_entries.name: Emergence
name_entries.felt_sense: When order appears without anyone planning it — and you need language for how complex outcomes arise from simple interactions
name_entries.definition: Complex patterns arising from simple rules and interactions
name_entries.why_it_matters: Understanding how transformation happens without blueprint

name_entries.name: Polycentricity
name_entries.felt_sense: When one center of authority can't handle the complexity — and you sense that multiple overlapping authorities might work better
name_entries.definition: Multiple centers of decision-making vs monocentric control
name_entries.why_it_matters: Governance principle from Ostrom for complex systems

name_entries.name: Cosmo-Local Production
name_entries.felt_sense: When knowledge should be global but manufacturing should be local — and you need a framework for that combination
name_entries.definition: Global knowledge shared locally manufactured
name_entries.why_it_matters: Model for replication without centralization
name_entries.key_concepts: Design global manufacture local, knowledge commons, local production

name_entries.name: Move at Speed of Trust
name_entries.felt_sense: When urgency pressure makes you skip relationship-building and everything falls apart — you needed a principle for pacing
name_entries.definition: Organizing paced by relationship-building not urgency
name_entries.why_it_matters: Principle for community organizing timeline

name_entries.name: Small is All
name_entries.felt_sense: When someone says 'but that's just one neighborhood' and you know that's exactly the point — large change through replication of small practice
name_entries.definition: Large-scale change through replication of small-scale practice
name_entries.why_it_matters: Emergent strategy principle for scaling

name_entries.name: Listening First
name_entries.felt_sense: When you arrive with solutions and realize the community already knows what it needs — you just hadn't listened yet
name_entries.definition: Community engagement begins with deep listening not solutions
name_entries.why_it_matters: Core principle for community-led work

name_entries.name: Consent vs Consensus
name_entries.felt_sense: When full agreement paralyzes a group and you need a decision method that asks 'can you live with this?' instead of 'do you love this?'
name_entries.definition: Decision-making when there are no paramount objections vs full agreement
name_entries.why_it_matters: Sociocratic principle for group decision-making
name_entries.key_concepts: Good enough for now safe enough to try, distributed authority, efficiency

name_entries.name: Theory U
name_entries.felt_sense: When you sense a future trying to emerge but can't get there through analysis alone — you need to feel your way into it
name_entries.definition: Process for sensing and actualizing emerging future
name_entries.why_it_matters: Methodology for collective sensing and change

name_entries.name: Emergent Strategy
name_entries.felt_sense: When top-down planning keeps failing and you realize transformation happens through adaptation, not blueprints
name_entries.definition: Small-scale practice creating conditions for transformation
name_entries.why_it_matters: Core methodology for adaptive organizing

name_entries.name: Just Transition
name_entries.felt_sense: When economic transformation is necessary but you know it will hurt people who are already hurting — you need a framework for equity in change
name_entries.definition: Framework for equitable shift from extractive to regenerative economy
name_entries.why_it_matters: Approach to economic transformation that centers frontline communities
name_entries.key_concepts: Climate justice, worker justice, community control

name_entries.name: Regenerative Development
name_entries.felt_sense: When sustainability feels like 'less bad' and you want development that actually increases the vitality of a place
name_entries.definition: Developmental approach to place-based change
name_entries.why_it_matters: Framework for transformation aligned with living systems
name_entries.key_concepts: Place-based change, living systems, developmental thinking

name_entries.name: Solidarity Economy
name_entries.felt_sense: When competition feels wrong but you can't articulate the alternative economic logic based on cooperation
name_entries.definition: Economic system based on cooperation and mutual aid
name_entries.why_it_matters: Alternative economic framework centered on solidarity not competition

name_entries.name: Community Wealth Building
name_entries.felt_sense: When economic development keeps benefiting outsiders and you want strategies that keep wealth circulating locally
name_entries.definition: Strategies for building locally-owned inclusive economy
name_entries.why_it_matters: Practical strategies for community-controlled economic development
name_entries.key_concepts: Local ownership, inclusive economy, democratic wealth

name_entries.name: Circular Economy
name_entries.felt_sense: When waste feels like a design failure and you want an economic model where everything cycles back
name_entries.definition: Economic system eliminating waste and continually using resources
name_entries.why_it_matters: Model for sustainable material flows and zero waste

name_entries.name: Doughnut Economics
name_entries.felt_sense: When GDP growth and planetary survival seem contradictory — and you need a framework that holds both human needs and ecological limits
name_entries.definition: Meeting human needs within planetary boundaries
name_entries.why_it_matters: Visual framework for sustainable and just economy

name_entries.name: Sociocracy
name_entries.felt_sense: When majority voting silences minorities and consensus takes forever — you need governance that asks 'is this good enough for now?'
name_entries.definition: Consent-based governance method
name_entries.why_it_matters: Method for democratic distributed governance

name_entries.name: Holacracy
name_entries.felt_sense: When hierarchy stifles but flatness creates chaos — you need structured self-organization
name_entries.definition: Organizational operating system distributing authority
name_entries.why_it_matters: Alternative to hierarchical organizational structure

name_entries.name: Ostrom's Commons Principles
name_entries.felt_sense: When people say 'commons always fail' and you know that's wrong but need the evidence to prove it
name_entries.definition: 8 principles for successful commons management
name_entries.why_it_matters: Evidence-based principles for commons governance

name_entries.name: Platform Cooperativism
name_entries.felt_sense: When you use a platform every day but have no ownership or voice — and you sense digital infrastructure should be democratically governed
name_entries.definition: Democratic ownership and governance of digital platforms
name_entries.why_it_matters: Framework for community-controlled digital infrastructure
name_entries.key_concepts: Worker ownership, data sovereignty, fair labor

name_entries.name: Prefigurative Politics
name_entries.felt_sense: When activists fighting for liberation reproduce the domination they're fighting — and you need a principle that the means must embody the ends
name_entries.definition: Organizing that embodies the world we want to create
name_entries.why_it_matters: Principle that infrastructure itself must embody desired values
name_entries.key_concepts: Means embody ends, direct action, alternative institutions

name_entries.name: Asset-Based Community Development
name_entries.felt_sense: When every needs assessment makes a community feel more broken — and you want to start from what's strong, not what's wrong
name_entries.definition: Building on community strengths not deficits
name_entries.why_it_matters: Language and approach centering community capacity not needs

name_entries.name: Trauma-Informed Practice
name_entries.felt_sense: When organizing keeps re-traumatizing the people it's trying to help — and you need practices that create safety first
name_entries.definition: Understanding impact of trauma in organizing and service
name_entries.why_it_matters: Approach for working with disaster-affected communities

source_entries.name: All About Love
source_entries.author: bell hooks
source_entries.description: Defines love as action and political practice rather than sentiment
source_entries.key_concepts: Love as political action, love requires justice

source_entries.name: Man's Search for Meaning
source_entries.author: Viktor Frankl
source_entries.description: Meaning must be found through action and attitude not given through framework
source_entries.key_concepts: Meaning through practice, logotherapy

source_entries.name: The Human Condition
source_entries.author: Hannah Arendt
source_entries.description: Loneliness as political condition created by loss of public realm
source_entries.key_concepts: Public realm, political action, loneliness as systemic

source_entries.name: Peace (Shalom)
source_entries.author: Walter Brueggemann
source_entries.description: Hebrew shalom as wholeness and right relationships not absence of conflict
source_entries.key_concepts: Shalom, economic justice, completeness

source_entries.name: Emergent Strategy
source_entries.author: adrienne maree brown
source_entries.description: Small-scale practice that creates conditions for emergence and transformation
source_entries.key_concepts: Fractals, emergence, small is all, move at speed of trust

source_entries.name: The Next American Revolution
source_entries.author: Grace Lee Boggs
source_entries.description: Revolution as returning to fundamental humanity not overthrowing systems
source_entries.key_concepts: Transformative organizing, place-based change, beloved community

source_entries.name: Small is Beautiful
source_entries.author: E.F. Schumacher
source_entries.description: Appropriate technology and economics as if people mattered
source_entries.key_concepts: Appropriate technology, Buddhist economics, human-scale

source_entries.name: Governing the Commons
source_entries.author: Elinor Ostrom
source_entries.description: Principles for successful commons management by communities
source_entries.key_concepts: Commons governance, Ostrom's 8 principles, polycentric governance

source_entries.name: Doughnut Economics

source_entries.description: Economic model meeting human needs within planetary boundaries
source_entries.key_concepts: Regenerative economics, safe and just space, circular economy
